title: A Short English Chronicle, from Lambeth MS. 306
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of U-M CME00112, which encodes James Gairdner's pp. 1–80 transcription in the 1880 Camden Society volume Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles. Gairdner transcribed Lambeth Palace Library MS 306, ff. 1r–46r: an abridged Brut, John Lydgate's Verses on the Kings of England copied as prose, and a London civic chronicle through 1465. The complete 1880 source volume is publicly downloadable and cached. Lambeth's complete 415-canvas manuscript facsimile is also publicly accessible and cached as a whole-object research bundle; exact work coverage is confirmed at canvases 5–95. Public access to Lambeth's photographs does not supply an open reuse license, and the provider's current forms require separate permission for publication or redistribution.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00112
entities.description: Pinned repository artifact representing the complete U-M encoding of Gairdner's printed pp. 1–80.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00112
entities.description: Public electronic text of Gairdner's complete Short English Chronicle section. The XML has one DIV1 and two DIV2 elements even though the second DIV2 contains both the Lydgate verses and London civic chronicle.

entities.label: James Gairdner, A Short English Chronicle, from Lambeth MS. 306 (1880)
entities.description: The immediate printed source for CME00112, occupying pp. 1–80 of Gairdner's complete Camden Society volume. The preface distinguishes the three consecutive medieval components and explains that later-hand corrections and annotations are reported in the edition.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: The inspected title page reads M.DCCC.LXXX. U-M's 1928 and Cambridge Core's 1881 assertions are recorded as discrepancies, not replacements.

bibliographic.citation: James Gairdner, ed., Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles, Camden Society, new series 28 (Westminster: J. B. Nichols and Sons, 1880), 'A Short English Chronicle, from Lambeth MS. 306,' pp. 1–80.
bibliographic.title: Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles, with Historical Memoranda by John Stowe, the Antiquary, and Contemporary Notes of Occurrences Written by Him in the Reign of Queen Elizabeth
bibliographic.publisher: J. B. Nichols and Sons for the Camden Society

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles (1880)
entities.description: Complete 246-page scan retaining the title page, Camden Society preliminaries, Gairdner's preface, all three edited chronicles, index, and binding context. The CME00112 work occupies printed pp. 1–80 / PDF pages 35–114.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF with OCR and item metadata derivatives

entities.label: London, Lambeth Palace Library, MS 306
entities.description: Composite paper codex of ten or eleven fascicles, bound circa 1504–1512 for T. Mylle and later owned and annotated by John Stow. In booklet 1, one anonymous Secretary hand copies the three CME00112 components circa 1465 on ff. 1r–46r: the abridged Brut at ff. 1r–17v, Lydgate at ff. 17v–18v, and the unique London Chronicle version at ff. 18v–46r.

date_statements.label: CME00112 components
date_statements.notes: The date applies to current catalog items 2–4 and Scribe A, not uniformly to all later contents or the early-sixteenth-century composite binding.

notes: Blind-stamped leather over wooden boards, rebacked; clasps missing.
Booklet 1 is ff. 1–48; items 2–4 are all assigned to Scribe A.

entities.label: Complete Lambeth MS 306 LUNA IIIF facsimile
entities.description: Public 415-canvas sequence covering the complete provider object from front cover through boards, end matter, and spine. CME00112 is confirmed at canvases 5–95, corresponding to ff. 1r–46r.
entities.reproduction_format: 415-canvas IIIF Presentation sequence cached at 1800-pixel derivative size except the native narrow spine image

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue record for MS 306
entities.description: Current institutional description of the composite codex and its individual items, including exact CME00112 folio ranges, date, hand, contents, bibliography, copies, and digital-surrogate link.

entities.label: DIMEV record 5731: Verses on the Kings of England (to Henry VI)
entities.description: Scholarly verse-index record identifying John Lydgate, IMEV/NIMEV 3632, the usual fifteen-stanza rhyme-royal form, subjects, witness census, and Lambeth MS 306 ff. 17v–18v as a prose-rendered and partly rewritten witness.

notes: The Brut is ff. 1r–17v / canvases 5–38; Lydgate is ff. 17v–18v / canvases 38–40; the London chronicle is ff. 18v–46r / canvases 40–95.
Shared folios and canvases at both transitions are genuine boundary surfaces.
Canvas 96 / f. 46v begins later John Stow material outside CME00112.
True hard-link reuse from the canonical complete MS 306 bundle; checksum, byte size, inode linkage, ZIP integrity, inventory count, image count, and start/end mapping were verified.

access.access_method: Use Lambeth's imaging form for supplied files and its separate permission form for publication or other reproduction.

local_copies.label: Lambeth digital photography order form
notes: Complete order form, but metadata-only relative to the manuscript facsimile entity.

local_copies.label: Lambeth permission to reproduce form
notes: Complete two-page permission form, but metadata-only relative to the manuscript facsimile entity.
The file opens successfully; qpdf reports a warning about a malformed linearization hint table, so no claim of qpdf-clean structure is made.
The forms describe supplied copies as private-study/research material and require separate, use-specific permission for publication or other reproduction.
The recorded service limit is 40 scans per order, with delivery potentially taking up to four weeks; whole-object or higher-resolution supply therefore requires direct consultation.

rights.component: Medieval Middle English textual content on ff. 1r–46r, excluding modern editions, encodings, catalog data, and photographs
rights.basis: The textual content was copied circa 1465 and predates modern copyright terms by centuries; this does not determine rights in modern representations.

rights.component: Gairdner's 1880 edition, preface, transcriptions, and editorial notes
rights.basis: The inspected edition was published in 1880, and the current U-M item labels the encoded source material Public Domain / No Copyright–United States.
notes: This statement does not license Lambeth's modern manuscript photographs.

rights.component: Public U-M digital-text representation of Gairdner's pp. 1–80
rights.basis: The current U-M item labels the material Public Domain and supplies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and derivatives as distinct digital reproductions
rights.jurisdiction: United States and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive metadata supplies no item-specific rights or license field. The underlying 1880 edition is separately recorded as public domain, but that does not justify inventing an open license for every provider-created file.

rights.component: Lambeth MS 306 digital photographs, IIIF presentation, and cached research bundle
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest supplies only 'LPL' as its rights value and no open license. Lambeth's current forms restrict supplied copies to private study/research and require separate, use-specific permission for publication or other reproduction.
notes: Public IIIF access and ignored local research caching do not authorize committing, redistributing, or publishing the photographs.
Underlying medieval textual content is separately public domain.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A composite manuscript containing many later works and John Stow materials beyond ff. 1r–46r
editorial_practices.representation: One named 80-page edition section transcribing the first three consecutive chronicles
editorial_practices.description: Gairdner selects the three same-hand circa-1465 items on ff. 1r–46r and prints them together as A Short English Chronicle, while his preface explicitly warns that the apparent single chronicle is really three.
notes: The section is not a facsimile or complete transcript of MS 306.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A corrupt and prose-rendered witness of Lydgate's verse
editorial_practices.representation: Fifteen prose paragraphs under the heading William Conquerour
editorial_practices.description: Gairdner identifies the passage as Lydgate's Verses on the Kings of England and says it is printed precisely as it stands in this manuscript rather than silently reconstructing regular verse lines.
notes: The schema's closest category is selection; the evidence concerns preservation of this witness's prose form, not a claim that Gairdner made an eclectic verse reconstruction.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete 1880 Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles volume
editorial_practices.representation: Only Gairdner's pp. 1–80 Short English Chronicle section
editorial_practices.description: CME00112 encodes all eighty printed pages of this section, not the complete 246-page scan or Gairdner's other edited chronicles and preliminaries.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three intellectual works printed consecutively on pp. 1–80
editorial_practices.representation: Two DIV2 elements; the second contains Lydgate P[1]–P[15] and the London chronicle from P[16] onward
editorial_practices.description: The encoding separates the Brut structurally but does not create a new DIV2 at the Lydgate-to-London boundary. The boundary remains recoverable from the incipit, paragraph position, printed page, manuscript folio, and current catalog description.
notes: The schema's closest practice category is selection; this record documents structural segmentation rather than an unexplained textual omission.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR; EEBO/IDG; TEXT; BODY/DIV1; DIV2[1–2]; PB, NOTE, TABLE, LG, and L elements
evidence.summary: Pins CME00112 at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c, Git blob ba56d8f76cb98d53b9bf16f77c21a45de74afd4d, and the recorded SHA-256. It supports the full pp. 1–80 selection, two-DIV2 structure, Middle English declaration, exact transitions, editorial notes, and raw XML counts.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00112, 'A short English chronicle from Lambeth MS. 306.'
evidence.locator: Item information, rights, contents, and full text
evidence.summary: Confirms the current title and identifier, Gairdner responsibility, public full text, headings, U-M's discrepant 1928 imprint statement, and the provider's Public Domain / No Copyright–United States assertion.

evidence.citation: James Gairdner, ed., Three Fifteenth-Century Chronicles, Camden Society, new series 28 (Westminster: J. B. Nichols and Sons, 1880), 'A Short English Chronicle, from Lambeth MS. 306,' pp. 1–80.
evidence.locator: Title page and colophon; preface pp. ii–iv; section pp. 1–80 = PDF pages 35–114
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms the title-page date M.DCCC.LXXX, editor, Camden Society publication, exact section extent, manuscript source, three-work boundary, same-pen observation, historical endpoint, Gairdner's valuation of the civic continuation, and his handling of later hands and the prose-rendered Lydgate text.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item threefifteenthce00gairrich metadata and complete derivatives.
evidence.summary: Confirms the item identifier, complete volume title, 1880 date, University of California Libraries contribution, available derivatives, and absence of an item-specific rights or license value in the cached metadata response.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue, MS 306, CHRONICLES, VERSE ETC.
evidence.locator: Whole composite; booklet 1; items 2–4; ff. 1r–46r; Scribe A; copies
evidence.summary: Supports the exact shelfmark, holding, physical codex, composite and binding history, John Stow provenance, same-hand circa-1465 production, three item descriptions and loci, PV-1400 classification, unique London Chronicle version, published Gairdner pages, microfilm copies, and digital-facsimile link.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA IIIF Presentation manifest for complete MS 306.
evidence.summary: Enumerates the complete 415-canvas provider object, labels, image services, and the bare LPL rights value. The cached ZIP contains all 415 images plus embedded manifest and inventory and preserves the whole object rather than only work pages.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection and integrity audit of Lambeth MS 306 boundaries in the complete cached IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: Bundle images 000005, 000038, 000040, 000095, and 000096; canvases 5, 38, 40, 95, and adjacent 96
evidence.summary: Confirms f. 1r / canvas 5 with the Cronycullys heading and opening; the Lydgate and London transitions on ff. 17v–18v / canvases 38–40; f. 46r / canvas 95 as the London chronicle endpoint; and different later Stow material on f. 46v / canvas 96. ZIP integrity, 415-image inventory parity, and complete-object endpoints were also checked.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Imaging Services, Digital Photography order form, and Permission to Reproduce form.
evidence.summary: Supports the current fee-based supply route, private-study/research condition, 40-scan order limit and delivery estimate, separate use-specific publication permission, nontransferable and format-specific authorization, acknowledgment requirements, and recorded contact details. Current 2021 form URLs match the cached bytes; older 2024 paths no longer do.
notes: The cached permission form opens as a two-page PDF; qpdf gives a warning about a malformed linearization hint table, so the evidence does not claim warning-free qpdf validation.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, record 5731, John Lydgate, Verses on the Kings of England (to Henry VI).
evidence.locator: Work description; Lambeth witness 31 at ff. 17v–18v
evidence.summary: Supports Lydgate authorship, DIMEV and IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, historical-poem and kings-of-England subjects, usual fifteen-stanza rhyme-royal form, witness heading, incipit, exact Lambeth locus, and the note that this witness is written as prose with its last stanza rewritten into prose.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Core, 'A Short English Chronicle, from Lambeth MS. 306,' Camden New Series, pp. 1–80.
evidence.summary: Confirms the modern DOI and pp. 1–80 section extent while exposing a September 1881 platform date that differs from the directly inspected 1880 volume title page.

open_questions.question: What catalog or reissue history produced U-M's 1928 date and Cambridge Core's 1881 date for a section whose inspected Camden Society title page is dated 1880?
next_steps: Trace the MARC or legacy UMDL source behind U-M's 1928 imprint statement.
Check Camden Society issue and distribution records and Cambridge's volume-level metadata for the 1880/1881 difference.
Continue to cite the inspected 1880 title page while preserving both later assertions as source-data discrepancies.

open_questions.question: Can the circa-1465 copying of Lambeth MS 306 items 2–4 be localized or institutionally contextualized without conflating scribal hand, authorship, exemplar origin, and later composite binding?
next_steps: Consult current codicological and palaeographical scholarship on booklet 1, Scribe A, and its paper stocks.
Evaluate any proposed government-department or civic-company context against direct evidence.
Do not create a place-of-compilation or named-compiler assertion from subject matter alone.

open_questions.question: What exact textual relationship does the Lambeth prose-rendered and partly rewritten copy have to the wider fifteen-stanza tradition of Lydgate's Verses on the Kings of England?
next_steps: Make a direct diplomatic transcription of ff. 17v–18v from canvases 38–40.
Collate it against the complete DIMEV witness census and a modern critical edition.
Keep exact verse-line extent unresolved until the corrupt prose witness can be reconstructed defensibly.

review_notes: Gairdner's 1880 edition is the immediate source for U-M CME00112; Lambeth MS 306 is the edition's direct manuscript source. The relation is supported at both printed-page and folio levels.
The complete Lambeth facsimile and complete Gairdner volume are preserved as full objects. Narrow work associations are carried by work_portion locators rather than by cropping the cached sources.
The three medieval components remain distinct even though Gairdner supplied one collective section heading and U-M used only two DIV2 elements.
Public-domain medieval text, public-domain 1880 edition, U-M's NoC-US assertion, Internet Archive file metadata, and Lambeth photograph permissions are separately scoped rights statements.
No access barrier was bypassed. Direct public IIIF resources were used; the viewer's verification interstitial is recorded rather than circumvented.
